label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out why smog has become a common concern. Let me read the passage again to understand the context. The passage starts by talking about how smog has covered parts of North China, with Beijing having only five days without smog in January. It mentions that rising PM2.5 levels scared people and that health experts link smog to more hospital visits for respiratory and heart issues. Then it shifts to polluted water and the need for environmental protection. The key part for the question is the part where it says: "As a popular online post said, air may be the only thing that is equal for everyone, despite your income or profession. People with higher incomes are able to drink only bottled spring water and eat only organic food by paying higher prices, but they breathe the same air as everyone else." This seems to highlight that smog affects everyone equally, regardless of their wealth or status because you can't avoid breathing the air.
